2606.08948.title: NutriMLLM: Multimodal Large Language Models for Dietary Micronutrient Analysis
2606.08948.paper_summary: Comprehensive estimation of dietary micronutrients from food images could improve clinical nutrition care, but training such models requires large multimodal datasets linking diverse foods to complete nutrient profiles. We first show that existing multimodal large language models (MLLMs), including leading proprietary models, are unreliable for this task. Across five model families and four independent evaluation benchmarks (ASA24, SNAPMe, FNDDS, and NutriBench), models frequently abstained or returned statistically implausible values. To address this gap without costly expert annotation, we repurposed a decade of population-scale 24-hour dietary recalls as structured prompts for text-to-image generation. This pipeline produced a synthetic corpus of about 1.1 million image-description-nutrient triplets, each pairing a generated food image with a complete 65-nutrient label. To our knowledge, this is the largest synthetic food-image corpus with comprehensive micronutrient annotation planned for public release upon publication. Fine-tuning Qwen3-VL (2B/4B/8B/30B) and GLM-4.6V-Flash on this corpus yielded NutriMLLM, the first family of vision-language models specialized for comprehensive dietary micronutrient estimation. We evaluate these models with a four-component framework that separately measures abstention, hallucination, overall usability, and per-nutrient numerical accuracy. On real food images, every NutriMLLM variant achieved near-complete coverage across all 65 nutrients, and the largest variant matched or exceeded proprietary baselines (GPT-5, Gemini 3, and Claude Sonnet 4.5) in accuracy on most nutrients. These results show that recall-driven synthetic supervision can make image-based comprehensive micronutrient estimation a tractable engineering problem and support dietary assessment, personalized nutrition guidance, and population-scale micronutrient surveillance.
2606.08948.paper_summary_zh: 全面估算飲食微量營養素從食物影像中可以改善臨床營養護理，但訓練這樣的模型需要大量的多模態數據集，將多樣的食物與完整的營養概況聯繫起來。我們首先顯示現有的多模態大型語言模型（MLLMs），包括領先的專有模型，在這項任務中不可靠。在五個模型家族和四個獨立評估基準（ASA24、SNAPMe、FNDDS 和 NutriBench）中，模型經常選擇不作答或返回統計上不合理的值。為了解決這一缺口而不需要昂貴的專家註釋，我們重新利用了十年的大規模24小時飲食回憶作為文本到影像生成的結構化提示。這個流程產生了約110萬個影像-描述-營養三元組的合成語料庫，每個三元組將生成的食物影像與完整的65種營養素標籤配對。據我們所知，這是計劃在發表後公開釋放的最大合成食物影像語料庫，並具有全面的微量營養素註釋。對這個語料庫進行微調的Qwen3-VL（2B/4B/8B/30B）和GLM-4.6V-Flash產生了NutriMLLM，這是第一個專門用於全面飲食微量營養素估算的視覺-語言模型家族。我們使用一個四組件框架來評估這些模型，分別測量不作答、幻覺、整體可用性和每種營養素的數值準確性。在真實的食物影像上，每個NutriMLLM變體在所有65種營養素上都達到了幾乎完全的覆蓋，而最大的變體在大多數營養素的準確性上與專有基準（GPT-5、Gemini 3 和 Claude Sonnet 4.5）相匹配或超過。這些結果表明，基於回憶的合成監督可以使基於影像的全面微量營養素估算成為一個可行的工程問題，並支持飲食評估、個性化營養指導和大規模微量營養素監測。
2606.08948.author: Runze Yan et.al.
2606.08948.authors: Runze Yan, Minxiao Wang, Jiaying Lu, Darren Liu, Xiao Hu, Hanqi Luo
